metadata.summary: Contextual, task-focused help in a layered hierarchy: inline hints, tooltips, FAQs, and guides. Reduce support costs and adoption friction.

content.title: Help and Documentation
content.summary: Users who cannot find answers give up, generating support costs and reducing adoption. Help content reduces friction only when it is discoverable at the moment of confusion, written in user language (not product-team language), and maintained in sync with the actual UI.

why.p: Users who cannot find answers give up, generating support costs and reducing adoption. Help content reduces friction only when it is discoverable at the moment of confusion, written in user language (not product-team language), and maintained in sync with the actual UI.

list.item: Writing or auditing in-app help content: tooltips, empty states, onboarding tours, inline hints
Generating knowledge base articles or how-to guides from product specs or changelogs
Auditing existing documentation for staleness, gaps, or coverage against support ticket patterns
Building a help search index from existing markdown/docs content
Creating contextual help copy for complex forms, settings pages, or error states
Replacing interface design improvements — if the UI requires a tooltip to be understood, the UI is broken first
Producing final user-facing copy without human editorial review (accuracy and tone)
Generating API reference documentation without code-level context (hallucination risk on technical details)
Substituting for user research on what users actually struggle with — support ticket analysis must precede content strategy

section.name: agent-integration-workflow
section.p: An agent ingests support ticket clusters (or user interview themes) and maps each to a help content type (tooltip, FAQ, how-to, onboarding step). It then generates draft content for each gap using the Help Content Template. A second agent runs a help audit pass against the existing docs index, producing a gap/staleness report. Output is a prioritized content backlog with draft content ready for human editorial review before publishing.
section.note: Recommended subagents: faion-sdd-executor-agent (runs structured help content generation tasks from an implementation-plan task file); General Claude subagent (opus) (analyzes support tickets and maps to help content gaps; handles nuanced user confusion patterns).

rule.statement: Help content must be generated only after support ticket analysis or user research identifies specific user confusion patterns. Agent-drafted content requires human editorial review before publishing.
rule.rationale: Generated documentation without ground-truth user research produces plausible-sounding but incorrect instructions. Step-by-step guides hallucinate features that do not exist or miss edge cases visible only in real support tickets.

rule.statement: Inline hints must be ≤15 words, tooltips ≤40 words, how-to articles must include a Result section describing what success looks like.
rule.rationale: Length constraints force clarity; tooltips longer than 40 words are rarely read. Result sections prevent user confusion about whether the task completed successfully.

example.title: Help Content Generation Prompt
example.code: You are a technical writer creating contextual help for [product feature]. Given this feature description: [description] Generate help content for three levels: 1. Inline hint (≤15 words) shown below the input field 2. Tooltip (≤40 words) shown on ? icon hover 3. How-to article (step-by-step, using the Help Content Template structure) Do not invent behavior not described. Flag any step where you need clarification.

example.title: Help Audit Prompt
example.code: Help audit prompt: Given this list of support tickets grouped by topic: [list] And this existing help content index: [index] For each ticket topic: 1. Does existing documentation cover it? (Y/N + link or "missing") 2. Is the content findable via search with natural language queries? 3. Is the content current (no references to deprecated features)? Output: a gap table with priority (High/Medium/Low) and recommended action.

section.name: where-it-fails
section.p: Help content created by agents can fail in several critical ways:

antipattern.description: Agents generate plausible but inaccurate step-by-step instructions when they lack ground-truth product screenshots or current UI state
antipattern.reason: Without access to the live product, agents cannot verify that steps match actual UI elements or account for real state transitions.

antipattern.description: Generated documentation goes stale immediately after product changes — no self-updating mechanism without a pipeline
antipattern.reason: Static documentation quickly diverges from reality; version-pinning screenshots and maintaining a content update schedule are manual processes.

antipattern.description: Contextual help placement requires UI/UX judgment that agents cannot make from text descriptions alone
antipattern.reason: Agents cannot visualize the layout; human designers must validate that help content appears in the right location without creating visual clutter.

antipattern.description: Agents cannot verify that help content is accessible (screen reader, keyboard navigation) without running automated tools
antipattern.reason: Accessibility must be tested with actual assistive technology; text descriptions alone cannot verify readability or usability for users with disabilities.

antipattern.description: Help audit tables generated from requirement lists miss implicit user confusion patterns — support tickets are a better input
antipattern.reason: Real users encounter edge cases and confusing state transitions that requirements documents do not capture.

section.name: cli-tools-and-services
section.p: The following CLI tools help manage and maintain help content:

list.item: mintlify: Docs-as-code platform with search and versioning (npm i -g mintlify / https://mintlify.com/docs)
docusaurus: Static docs site with full-text search (npx create-docusaurus@latest / https://docusaurus.io)
vale: Prose linter — enforces plain language, style guide rules (brew install vale / https://vale.sh)
markdownlint-cli: Validates markdown structure and consistency (npm i -g markdownlint-cli / https://github.com/igorshubovych/markdownlint-cli)
broken-link-checker: Finds dead links in documentation (npm i -g broken-link-checker / https://github.com/stevenvachon/broken-link-checker)

section.p: SaaS and OSS services for help content delivery:

list.item: Intercom Articles (SaaS, REST API): Create/update help articles programmatically; search analytics included
Notion (SaaS, REST API): Draft help content in Notion pages via API; export to publishing pipeline
Mintlify (SaaS/OSS, CLI + API): Git-based docs with AI search; agents can open PRs for doc updates
Algolia DocSearch (SaaS, REST API): Power help search with crawled docs; query analytics reveal missing content
Chameleon / Pendo (SaaS, Partial): In-app guided tours; REST APIs for flow management; no agent-authoring SDK
ReadMe.com (SaaS, REST API): API docs + guides with version management; supports programmatic content sync

section.name: help-audit-script
section.p: Inline script to scan markdown docs for broken internal links and stale screenshots:
section.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash # help-audit.sh — check docs dir for broken links and outdated markers # Usage: bash help-audit.sh docs/ DOCS=${1:-docs} echo "=== Broken internal links ===" grep -rEo '\[.*?\]\(([^)]+)\)' "$DOCS" \ | grep -v 'http' \ | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' \ | tr -d ')' \ | while read -r link; do [ ! -f "$DOCS/$link" ] && echo "MISSING: $link" done
echo "" echo "=== Potentially stale content (references to version markers) ===" grep -rn "TODO\|FIXME\|outdated\|deprecated\|v[0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+" \ --include="*.md" "$DOCS" \ | head -40

list.item: Write help content at the moment a feature ships, not retroactively — treat it as a definition of done
Use support ticket language, not product team language — users search with their words, not yours
Every how-to article must have a Result section describing what success looks like
Empty states are help content — design them with action guidance, not just "No items found"
Version-pin screenshots with the app version they were taken from; stale screenshots erode trust faster than missing ones
Track zero-results help searches weekly — each is an explicit content gap signal

section.name: agent-gotchas
section.p: When working with agents on help content generation, be aware of these common failure modes:

antipattern.description: Agents cannot verify that generated step-by-step instructions match the actual current UI
antipattern.reason: Always require a human QA pass against the live product before publishing.

antipattern.description: Generated FAQ answers are often correct in aggregate but wrong in specific edge cases
antipattern.reason: Human review required before publishing; test with actual users if possible.

antipattern.description: Agents producing onboarding tour copy do not know which UI elements exist or their current labels
antipattern.reason: Provide a component inventory as input; require verification against the running product.

antipattern.description: Direct publication of agent-generated documentation without editorial review
antipattern.reason: Treat all agent output as first draft requiring editorial review before publishing.

antipattern.description: Agents will invent plausible troubleshooting steps that do not correspond to real error states
antipattern.reason: Require a list of actual error codes/states as input to the agent prompt.

template.purpose: Step-by-step how-to article structure with overview, steps, result, troubleshooting, and related topics
Audit table for help availability, common user questions, content quality, gaps, and priorities

script.purpose: Bash script to check docs directory for broken internal links and stale content markers
